world_name: Legend of the Five Rings - Rokugan
prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG Japanese feudal fantasy atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Legend of the Five Rings / Rokugan inspired Japanese feudal fantasy map, continent plus archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view of one dominant samurai-empire continent with a large uneven offshore archipelago.
Subject: one main continent with complex coastlines, misty gulfs, bays, capes, narrow straits, broken rocky coasts, river deltas, inland lakes, and many irregular island chains offshore. Islands must be unevenly distributed with varied sizes and shapes: castle islands, tiny shrine islets, craggy islands, reef rings, lagoons, narrow channels, remote atolls, spirit-realm islands, pirate coves, sacred forest islands, and Shadowlands-tainted forbidden islands. Include Rokugan-like provinces, celestial mountain ranges, corrupted southern border, castle districts, sacred groves, rice plains, cherry blossom valleys, volcanic hills, wetlands, tundra highlands, forests, river basins, and coastal ports.
Style/medium: Rokugan map, Japanese feudal empire provinces, elemental rings aesthetic, mon crest-like non-text symbols, ink-and-watercolor atlas, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook cartography.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, dominant continent offset to one side and archipelago sweeping diagonally around it, no simple grid spacing, full map visible.
Lighting/mood: honorable samurai epic, cherry blossom atmosphere, clan politics, court intrigue, elemental spirits, secret sea routes, shadowlands corruption at the edge of the world.
Color palette: washi parchment, sumi ink black, muted jade green, cherry blossom pink, imperial red, gold accents, mist blue sea, shadowlands sickly grey-green.
Materials/textures: washi paper, brush ink coastlines, watercolor washes, delicate mon-like symbols without readable characters, mineral pigment terrain.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable Japanese or Chinese characters, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Islands should feel natural and irregular, not evenly spaced.
